Unlike DATA-R01's wholesale withholding of specs due to an unresolved variant conflict, the current official page directly states usable per-variant numbers for most fields; only fields that genuinely vary by variant (DoF, runtime, speed) are recorded per-variant rather than forced into one number.

documentsReviewed.title: TechShare G1(開発版)独占販売権取得・G1-D予約販売開始のお知らせ

evidence.value: Standard = fixed vertical column (no locomotion, i.e. stationary); Flagship = adds a mobile base with 360° in-place rotation (wheeled/mobile)
evidence.normalizedValue: Standard = fixed vertical column (no locomotion, i.e. stationary); Flagship = adds a mobile base with 360° in-place rotation (wheeled/mobile)
evidence.evidenceSummary: Official page: Standard is a fixed-stand configuration; Flagship adds "mobile baseplate functionality" and max speed 1.5m/s with 360° in-place rotation. A single mobility value cannot represent both variants under one robotId, so this is flagged for a variant-level split rather than discarding the information.

evidence.value: 1260-1680mm column vertical-travel range (Standard footprint 620x620mm; Flagship footprint 525x570mm)
evidence.normalizedValue: 1260-1680mm column vertical-travel range (Standard footprint 620x620mm; Flagship footprint 525x570mm)
evidence.evidenceSummary: Both variants share the same 1260-1680mm column lift range; footprint differs by variant. This is a lift range, not a fixed standing height like the biped models.

evidence.value: About 90kg (with battery), both Standard and Flagship
evidence.evidenceSummary: Current official page states ~90kg for both variants; see conflicts note re: an earlier discrepancy with a prior TechShare page version.

evidence.value: 1.5 m/s (Flagship only; Standard has no locomotion)
evidence.evidenceSummary: Official page states Flagship max speed 1.5m/s with 360° in-place rotation; Standard is a fixed stand with no mobility spec.

evidence.value: Standard: 17 DoF (excl. end-effector); Flagship: 19 DoF (excl. end-effector)
evidence.normalizedValue: Standard: 17 DoF (excl. end-effector); Flagship: 19 DoF (excl. end-effector)
evidence.evidenceSummary: Official page states both figures explicitly and unambiguously per variant; recorded as a per-variant pair rather than a single number.

payloadKg.note: No generic payload figure; see loadRatings (Approx. 3kg arm payload, both variants).

evidence.normalizedValue: Standard: About 2h; Flagship: About 6h
evidence.evidenceSummary: Official page states both figures explicitly per variant (Flagship adds a built-in 30Ah chassis battery on top of the shared 9Ah upper-body battery).

batteryCapacityWh.note: Official page states Ah figures (9Ah upper-body quick-release, both variants; +30Ah built-in chassis battery, Flagship only) but no voltage/Wh value, so Wh is not derived.

evidence.value: Upper-body 9Ah quick-release battery (both variants); Flagship additionally has a built-in 30Ah chassis battery
evidence.normalizedValue: Upper-body 9Ah quick-release battery (both variants); Flagship additionally has a built-in 30Ah chassis battery
evidence.evidenceSummary: Base battery system is common; Flagship adds a second chassis battery, noted in the summary.

evidence.value: Teleoperation with <100ms system latency at 60Hz sampling; secondary development supported
evidence.normalizedValue: Teleoperation with <100ms system latency at 60Hz sampling; secondary development supported
evidence.evidenceSummary: Official control/latency spec section, common to both variants.

evidence.normalizedValue: Unitree SDK2 (C++) / Python interface
evidence.evidenceSummary: GitHub org repo coverage description explicitly lists G1-D among supported robot models.

evidence.value: 8-core high-performance CPU; optional high-power modules (e.g., Orin NX 16GB, 100TOPS)
evidence.normalizedValue: 8-core high-performance CPU; optional high-power modules (e.g., Orin NX 16GB, 100TOPS)
evidence.evidenceSummary: Official compute spec, common to both variants.

loadRatings.condition: Official "Approx. 3kg" arm payload, both Standard and Flagship; manufacturer notes it varies with arm extension posture.

priceFallback.note: Neither the Unitree official page nor TechShare's reservation-sales press release (2025-12-01) discloses a price; TechShare directs interested parties to contact its sales team (TEL 03-5683-7293 / unitree@techshare.co.jp).

currentFeatures.feature: End-to-end platform emphasis: data collection, model training (imitation/reinforcement learning), and inference
Optional end-effectors: 2-finger gripper / 3-finger dexterous hand (with and without tactile) / 5-finger dexterous hand
Flagship-only chassis sensors: LiDAR, 2x depth cameras, 2x collision sensors, 2x obstacle-detection sensors
Teleoperation with <100ms system latency at 60Hz sampling
OTA updates and secondary-development support

useCaseGaps.officialExpression: Target application domains stated as four generic categories: Service / Life / Retail / Industry (no specific named task within each)
useCaseGaps.reason: Too generic to map onto a specific existing published UseCase without inference; recorded as a gap rather than force-connected.

japanDistributors: TechShare (exclusive domestic distribution rights for Unitree G1 development version; reservation sales for G1-D opened 2025-12-01)
procurement.supportNote: TechShare is the reservation-sales/support contact in Japan; pricing and maintenance/warranty terms require direct inquiry.

conflicts.description: DATA-R01 (checked 2026-07-16) cited a TechShare G1-D page showing Standard ~50kg / "Ultimate" ~80kg, conflicting with Unitree's official ~90kg for both variants. Re-checking the TechShare G1-D product page today (2026-07-17) found no weight or price figures currently displayed there at all, so the previously-cited conflicting numbers could not be reproduced. Given the current official Unitree page unambiguously states ~90kg for both Standard and Flagship, this record uses that figure as family-common rather than continuing to withhold all G1-D specs as DATA-R01 did.

unresolved: Naming mismatch: Unitree's own official page uses "Standard/Flagship"; TechShare's Japan press release uses "Standard/Ultimate" (vehicle mobility + elevation) for what appears to be the same two-tier split. The Flagship↔Ultimate mapping is inferred from context, not explicitly confirmed as identical by either source.
humanReviewRequired: Confirm with Unitree/TechShare whether "Flagship" (official) and "Ultimate" (TechShare) refer to the exact same configuration before using them interchangeably in buyer-facing copy.
